sanskrit:
न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मनः।
निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव।।
transliteration:
na ca śaknomy avasthātuṃ bhramatīva ca me manaḥ |
nimittāni ca paśyāmi viparītāni keśava ||
literal_meaning: I am unable to stand here any longer; my mind is reeling as if spinning; I see only ominous signs that are the opposite of what I hoped for, O Keśava.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s nervous system is overwhelmed: his legs tremble, his cognition dissolves into catastrophic forecasting, and every future scenario appears inverted—victory feels like defeat, duty feels like murder. This is the moment when a lifetime of moral certainties collapses into raw existential dread, revealing how tightly our sense of self is moored to outcomes and social roles.
psychological_state: A high-functioning individual suddenly paralyzed by the realization that every projected future leads to irreversible moral harm and self-betrayal.
trigger_scenario: A surgeon about to operate on her own child, a CEO poised to sign a merger that will enrich shareholders but destroy hometown jobs, or a soldier ordered to fire into a crowd—moments when professional identity and personal conscience violently collide.
anchor_text: When certainty crumbles, steady yourself by shifting focus from outcomes to the integrity of your action.
